commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh You search out my path and my lying down, and are acquainted with all my ways. Before they were formed. Chrysostom) Line. Or the measure of my life and actions. (Calmet) Hebrew, "my lying down "alluding to hares, which are followed to their resting places. (Houbigant) Some read, "my direction "(St. Hilary) or "limit. Augustine) (Calmet) The utmost extent of my intention, and the words yet unuttered, are not unknown to thee. (Worthington).
